anti_claims: This skill JSON seed does not flip source authority away from the current skill markdown projection.
Resolved operated-standard and acted-on-kind edges are structural route mappings, not proof the skill is complete or used correctly by agents.
Absent mechanism or concept neighbours are residual pressure, not evidence that no such neighbours exist.
Generated graph, health, and atlas projections cannot be read back as source evidence.

omitted: mechanism or concept neighbours not named by typed source mapping
omission_receipt.reason: Skill instance preserves public-safe markdown source identity, digest, and typed route mapping only. JSON parity does not prove full skill-body completeness or runtime correctness.

residual_pressure.reentry_condition: Bind skill.uses mechanism and skill.applies concept edges only when source evidence names ids.

justification.summary: The standard JSON at standards/std_microcosm_external_candidate.json declares skills.author.skill_id=skill.microcosm.external_candidate.author with status planned and purpose 'create a valid External candidate instance'. The same standard defines external_candidate as provider, annex, feed, prompt, or memory payload before local authority adoption with rebuild_action=defer and a projection/public-private boundary; concept.import_projection_and_drift_control_bundle names source import and projection drift controls as provenance-preserving boundaries. This markdown source row binds the planned triad route and that concept edge without upgrading the standard from public_microcosm_standard_v1/draft or inferring mechanism neighbours.

The standard JSON at standards/std_microcosm_external_candidate.json declares skills.author.skill_id=skill.microcosm.external_candidate.author with status planned and purpose 'create a valid External candidate instance'. The same standard defines external_candidate as provider, annex, feed, prompt, or memory payload before local authority adoption with rebuild_action=defer and a projection/public-private boundary; concept.import_projection_and_drift_control_bundle names source import and projection drift controls as provenance-preserving boundaries. This markdown source row binds the planned triad route and that concept edge without upgrading the standard from public_microcosm_standard_v1/draft or inferring mechanism neighbours.
Skill source mapping names this concept as an applied doctrine boundary.

unpopulated_selective_relations.reason: Skill markdown source does not name used mechanism ids.

The same standard defines external_candidate as provider, annex, feed, prompt, or memory payload before local authority adoption with rebuild_action=defer and a projection/public-private boundary; concept.import_projection_and_drift_control_bundle names source import and projection drift controls as provenance-preserving boundaries. This markdown source row binds the planned triad route and that concept edge without upgrading the standard from public_microcosm_standard_v1/draft or inferring mechanism neighbours.

title: Microcosm External Candidate Author
triggers: The External candidate standard declares this author skill id with status planned.
A governed skill source row is needed before standard.owns_triad.skill can resolve without treating generated projections as authority.
workflow: Read std_microcosm_external_candidate JSON, its authority ceiling, validator refs, receipt refs, and lattice health before editing external_candidate artifacts.
Author only source-backed public fields for the governed instance, including authority boundary, validator expectations, receipt expectations, and anti-claims.
Regenerate the skill corpus, bind the import/projection-drift concept edge, and preserve missing mechanism neighbours as residual pressure unless a source mapping names them.
